---
title: "About"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
---

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}

```{=html}
<div class="about-hero">
  <p class="about-eyebrow">ABOUT</p>
  <h1 class="about-title">Engineering the<br/>future of AI education.</h1>
  <p class="about-tagline">An open-source, principles-first curriculum for understanding how machine learning systems are built, optimized, and deployed at every scale.</p>
</div>
```

## Mission {#mission}

```{=html}
<div class="about-section">
```

The world is rushing to build AI systems. It is not engineering them.

Machine learning has transformed every industry, but the education pipeline has not kept pace. Students learn algorithms in isolation, divorced from the silicon that executes them, the memory hierarchies that constrain them, and the networks that distribute them. The result: a generation of practitioners who can call `model.fit()` but cannot reason about *why* their model is slow, *where* the bottleneck lives, or *how* to fix it without guessing.

**Machine Learning Systems** exists to close that gap. We teach AI as infrastructure — governed by physical laws, shaped by engineering constraints, and measurable through quantitative reasoning. Our thesis is simple: *constraints drive architecture.* You do not choose a Transformer because it is trendy; you choose it because of how it parallelizes on real silicon.

```{=html}
<div class="pillar-grid">
  <div class="pillar-card">
    <div class="pillar-icon">⚙️</div>
    <h3>Principles Over APIs</h3>
    <p>We teach the physics beneath the framework. Memory hierarchies, not <code>torch.cuda()</code>. Arithmetic intensity, not library calls. Knowledge that endures beyond any single tool.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="pillar-card">
    <div class="pillar-icon">📐</div>
    <h3>Quantitative Reasoning</h3>
    <p>Every claim is backed by a number. Students learn to estimate, measure, and verify — the same skills that separate a systems engineer from a script runner.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="pillar-card">
    <div class="pillar-icon">🌍</div>
    <h3>Open and Global</h3>
    <p>Free HTML, PDF, and EPUB. Used at universities across six continents. Licensed under CC-BY-NC-SA 4.0 so anyone can teach from it, adapt it, and build on it.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="pillar-card">
    <div class="pillar-icon">🔬</div>
    <h3>Full-Stack Curriculum</h3>
    <p>Not just a textbook. TinyTorch, hardware kits, interactive labs, lecture slides, and instructor resources — everything needed to teach a two-semester course.</p>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="stats-strip">
  <div class="stat-item">
    <span class="stat-number" id="stat-stars">22,800+</span>
    <span class="stat-label">GitHub Stars</span>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-item">
    <span class="stat-number">2</span>
    <span class="stat-label">Volumes</span>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-item">
    <span class="stat-number">32</span>
    <span class="stat-label">Chapters</span>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-item">
    <span class="stat-number" id="stat-contributors">90+</span>
    <span class="stat-label">Contributors</span>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-item">
    <span class="stat-number">6</span>
    <span class="stat-label">Continents</span>
  </div>
</div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<hr class="about-divider" />
```

## Team {#team}

```{=html}
<div class="about-section">
```

Machine Learning Systems is a collaborative effort led by Harvard University and shaped by contributors spanning academia, industry, and the global TinyML community.

```{=html}
<div class="team-grid">
  <div class="team-card">
    <img src="https://github.com/profvjreddi.png" alt="Vijay Janapa Reddi" loading="lazy" />
    <h3>Vijay Janapa Reddi</h3>
    <div class="team-role">Lead Author</div>
    <div class="team-affiliation">Associate Professor, Harvard University</div>
  </div>
  <div class="team-card">
    <img src="https://github.com/Mjrovai.png" alt="Marcelo Rovai" loading="lazy" />
    <h3>Marcelo Rovai</h3>
    <div class="team-role">Hardware Kits Lead</div>
    <div class="team-affiliation">Professor, UNIFEI</div>
  </div>
  <div class="team-card">
    <img src="https://github.com/hzeljko.png" alt="Zeljko Herold" loading="lazy" />
    <h3>Zeljko Herold</h3>
    <div class="team-role">Core Contributor</div>
    <div class="team-affiliation">Community</div>
  </div>
  <div class="team-card">
    <img src="https://github.com/jasonjabbour.png" alt="Jason Jabbour" loading="lazy" />
    <h3>Jason Jabbour</h3>
    <div class="team-role">Core Contributor</div>
    <div class="team-affiliation">Harvard University</div>
  </div>
  <div class="team-card">
    <img src="https://github.com/colbybanbury.png" alt="Colby Banbury" loading="lazy" />
    <h3>Colby Banbury</h3>
    <div class="team-role">MLPerf / Benchmarks</div>
    <div class="team-affiliation">Harvard University</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

### Academic Network

The curriculum builds on the broader [TinyMLedu Open Education Initiative](https://tinyml.seas.harvard.edu), a global partnership with the tinyML Foundation, ICTP, Google, Arduino, and Edge Impulse. The TinyML4D Academic Network connects 40+ universities across six continents, sharing course materials and conducting collaborative research.

```{=html}
</div>
<hr class="about-divider" />
```

## Contributors {#contributors}

```{=html}
<div class="about-section">
```

This curriculum is built by an open-source community. Every chapter, diagram, lab, and code review reflects the expertise of contributors from around the world.

```{=html}
<div class="contributors-grid" id="contributors-grid">
  <!-- Populated by JavaScript from contributors.json or statically -->
  <noscript>
    <p>View all contributors on <a href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/cs249r_book#contributors-">GitHub</a>.</p>
  </noscript>
</div>

<p style="text-align: center; margin-top: 1.5rem;">
  <a href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/cs249r_book#contributors-" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="cta-btn-secondary" style="font-size: 0.85rem; padding: 0.5rem 1.5rem;">
    View all contributors on GitHub →
  </a>
</p>

<script>
// Fetch contributors from GitHub API (with fallback)
async function loadContributors() {
  const grid = document.getElementById('contributors-grid');
  try {
    // Try local data file first (generated by CI)
    let data;
    try {
      const localResp = await fetch('contributors.json');
      if (localResp.ok) {
        data = await localResp.json();
      }
    } catch(e) { /* fall through to API */ }

    // Fallback to GitHub API
    if (!data) {
      const resp = await fetch('https://api.github.com/repos/harvard-edge/cs249r_book/contributors?per_page=100');
      if (!resp.ok) throw new Error('API failed');
      data = await resp.json();
    }

    // Filter bots, render avatars
    const contributors = (data.contributors || data)
      .filter(c => !c.login.includes('[bot]') && c.login !== 'dependabot')
      .slice(0, 60);

    grid.innerHTML = contributors.map(c => `
      <a href="${c.html_url}" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="contributor-avatar" title="${c.login}">
        <img src="${c.avatar_url}&s=96" alt="${c.login}" loading="lazy" width="48" height="48" />
        <span class="contributor-tooltip">${c.name || c.login}</span>
      </a>
    `).join('');
  } catch(e) {
    grid.innerHTML = '<p style="color: var(--ab-text-muted); font-size: 0.9rem;">View contributors on <a href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/cs249r_book#contributors-">GitHub</a>.</p>';
  }
}
loadContributors();
</script>
</div>
<hr class="about-divider" />
```

## Adopters {#adopters}

```{=html}
<div class="about-section">
```

Machine Learning Systems is taught at universities and used by organizations worldwide. If your institution uses this curriculum, [let us know](https://github.com/harvard-edge/cs249r_book/discussions) — we would love to feature you here.

```{=html}
<div class="adopter-grid">
  <div class="adopter-card">
    <div class="adopter-name">Harvard University</div>
    <div class="adopter-detail">CS 249r · Cambridge, MA</div>
  </div>
  <div class="adopter-card">
    <div class="adopter-name">Barnard College, Columbia</div>
    <div class="adopter-detail">Embedded ML · New York, NY</div>
  </div>
  <div class="adopter-card">
    <div class="adopter-name">UNIFEI</div>
    <div class="adopter-detail">TinyML · Itajubá, Brazil</div>
  </div>
  <div class="adopter-card">
    <div class="adopter-name">ICTP</div>
    <div class="adopter-detail">SciTinyML · Trieste, Italy</div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="cta-banner" style="margin-top: 2rem;">
  <h3>Teaching ML Systems?</h3>
  <p>Our Instructor Hub provides everything you need: syllabi, assessment rubrics, lecture slides, and TA resources for a two-semester curriculum.</p>
  <div class="cta-buttons">
    <a href="https://mlsysbook.ai/instructors/" class="cta-btn-primary">The Blueprint →</a>
    <a href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/cs249r_book/discussions" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="cta-btn-secondary">Join the Discussion</a>
  </div>
</div>
</div>
<hr class="about-divider" />
```

## Press {#press}

```{=html}
<div class="about-section">
```

Selected milestones and coverage for the Machine Learning Systems curriculum.

```{=html}
<ul class="press-list">
  <li>
    <span class="press-icon"><i class="fas fa-book"></i></span>
    <div class="press-content">
      <h3>MIT Press Publication</h3>
      <p>Two-volume hardcover edition forthcoming from MIT Press, Summer 2026.</p>
      <span class="press-date">2026</span>
    </div>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="press-icon"><i class="fas fa-star"></i></span>
    <div class="press-content">
      <h3>22,800+ GitHub Stars</h3>
      <p>Among the most-starred open-source textbook repositories in machine learning and systems engineering.</p>
      <span class="press-date">March 2026</span>
    </div>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="press-icon"><i class="fas fa-award"></i></span>
    <div class="press-content">
      <h3>IEEE Citation</h3>
      <p>Formally citable as an IEEE publication for academic research and coursework.</p>
      <span class="press-date">2024</span>
    </div>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="press-icon"><i class="fas fa-globe"></i></span>
    <div class="press-content">
      <h3>Global Reach via TinyMLedu</h3>
      <p>Curriculum materials used across 40+ universities and six continents through the TinyML4D Academic Network and ICTP partnership.</p>
      <span class="press-date">2021–present</span>
    </div>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="press-icon"><i class="fas fa-graduation-cap"></i></span>
    <div class="press-content">
      <h3>edX Professional Certificate</h3>
      <p>Foundational content contributed to the HarvardX TinyML Professional Certificate on edX, completed by thousands of learners worldwide.</p>
      <span class="press-date">2020–present</span>
    </div>
  </li>
</ul>
</div>
<hr class="about-divider" />
```

## License {#license}

```{=html}
<div class="about-section">
```

Machine Learning Systems is released under the [Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International License](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/) (CC-BY-NC-SA 4.0).

This means you are free to share and adapt the material for non-commercial purposes, as long as you give appropriate credit and distribute your contributions under the same license.

```{=html}
<div class="license-badge">
  <i class="fab fa-creative-commons"></i>
  <i class="fab fa-creative-commons-by"></i>
  <i class="fab fa-creative-commons-nc"></i>
  <i class="fab fa-creative-commons-sa"></i>
  <span>CC-BY-NC-SA 4.0</span>
</div>

<div class="cta-banner">
  <h3>Get involved</h3>
  <p>Whether you are a student, educator, researcher, or engineer — there is a place for you in this community.</p>
  <div class="cta-buttons">
    <a href="https://mlsysbook.ai/vol1/" class="cta-btn-primary">Start Reading →</a>
    <a href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/cs249r_book" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="cta-btn-secondary">Contribute on GitHub</a>
    <a href="https://mlsysbook.ai/newsletter/" class="cta-btn-secondary">Subscribe to Newsletter</a>
  </div>
</div>
</div>
```

:::
